rules.description: Counts column separators per row and flags rows whose width differs from the modal column count of the table.
rules.why_it_indicates_an_error: Merged or split cells in the PDF extraction stage produce rows with fewer or more columns than the header, indicating column boundary errors.

rules.description: Detects cells containing multiple distinct values concatenated without a separator (e.g., 'Waived Waived' or 'HK$150 HK$125 HK$100').
rules.why_it_indicates_an_error: When the PDF extractor fails to maintain column boundaries, values from multiple tier columns collapse into a single cell.

rules.description: Flags cells containing garbled or implausible text that bears no resemblance to surrounding content (e.g., 'lakada ada fara', ')A/ : 1').
rules.why_it_indicates_an_error: OCR artefacts from non-text PDF regions (logos, rules, watermarks) can inject random character sequences into extracted table cells.

rules.description: In a column whose header or majority of cells contain numeric/currency patterns (HK$, RMB, %, digits), flags cells that are unexpectedly empty.
rules.why_it_indicates_an_error: Column-shift or extraction failures can leave numeric cells blank while their values land in the wrong column.

rules.description: Flags tables whose column count is higher than expected, suggesting the extractor inserted phantom column boundaries.
rules.why_it_indicates_an_error: PDF extraction can mistake whitespace or alignment guides for column separators, generating extra empty columns.
